value.reviewer_notes: =Reviewer requested edits. Update final_reply in Google Sheets before sending.
Reviewer rejected the AI suggested reply. No email was sent.

parameters.content:
## Slack Button Webhook

Receives Slack button clicks.

Slack Interactivity Request URL should point to this workflow’s production webhook.

Use production URL when active:
.../webhook/support-approval

## Parse Slack Action

Extracts the reviewer decision from Slack.

Outputs:
- ticket_id
- decision
- clicked_by_name
- slack_channel
- slack_message_ts

Button value format:
ticket_id|decision

## Parse Slack Action

Finds the matching ticket in Google Sheets using ticket_id & relevant column

## Prepare Ticket Context

Combines Google Sheets ticket data with Slack reviewer data.

Creates shared fields used by all decision paths:
- reply_to_send
- reviewed_by
- reviewed_at
- slack_channel
- slack_message_ts

## Route Decision

Routes based on the Slack button clicked.

approve → send reply
needs_edit → mark for editing
reject → reject AI draft

Only approve sends an email.

## Approve Path

Reviewer approved the AI draft.

Actions:
1. Send Gmail reply
2. Mark ticket as Sent
3. Update original Slack message

Customer receives the email only in this path.

## Send Gmail Reply

Sends the approved final_reply to the customer.

Uses:
- customer_email
- subject
- reply_to_send

This node runs only after Slack approval.

## Update Ticket as Sent

Updates the Google Sheet after the Gmail reply is sent.

Sets:
- status = Sent
- approved_by
- approved_at
- sent_at

## Update Slack: Approved

Updates the original Slack message using chat.update.

Shows:
- Approved and sent
- ticket_id
- approver
- sent reply summary

This removes the approval buttons.

## Needs Edit Path

Reviewer wants to revise the AI draft.

No email is sent.

The ticket is marked Needs Edit so a human can update final_reply in Google Sheets.

## Update Ticket as Needs Edit

Updates Google Sheets when the reviewer requests edits.

Sets:
- status = Needs Edit
- reviewer_notes
- approved_by / reviewed_by
- approved_at / reviewed_at

## Update Slack: Needs Edit

Updates the original Slack message.

Shows that the ticket needs editing and reminds the reviewer to update final_reply in Google Sheets.

Buttons are removed.

## Reject Path

Reviewer rejects the AI draft.

No email is sent.

Use this when the AI response is wrong, risky, irrelevant, or should be handled manually.

## Update Ticket as Rejected

Updates Google Sheets when the AI draft is rejected.

Sets:
- status = Rejected
- reviewer_notes
- approved_by / reviewed_by
- approved_at / reviewed_at

## Update Slack: Rejected

Updates the original Slack message to show rejection.

Confirms no email was sent and removes the buttons.

## Safety

Only the Approve path sends a customer email.

Needs Edit and Reject never send Gmail replies.

This keeps the AI human-in-the-loop and prevents unsafe automatic responses.

## Support Approval Button Handler

This workflow handles the human decision after the AI triage workflow posts a Slack approval message.

When a reviewer clicks Approve, Needs Edit, or Reject in Slack, this workflow receives the button event, finds the matching ticket in Google Sheets, and updates the ticket based on the reviewer’s decision.

Only the Approve path sends a Gmail reply to the customer.
Needs Edit and Reject never send emails.

## Workflow Steps

1. Receive Slack button click through Webhook.
2. Parse the Slack action to get ticket_id and decision.
3. Look up the matching ticket in Google Sheets.
4. Prepare shared ticket and reviewer context.
5. Route the decision:
   - Approve → send Gmail reply, mark Sent, update Slack.
   - Needs Edit → mark Needs Edit, update Slack.
   - Reject → mark Rejected, update Slack.
6. Update the original Slack message and remove the buttons.
